description: Founded 17 May 1961 by [Mehdi Bazargan](/people/mehdi-bazargan), [Mahmoud Taleghani](/people/mahmoud-taleghani), and [Yadollah Sahabi](/people/yadollah-sahabi) as a religiously oriented offshoot of the [National Front](/orgs/national-front-iran). Oldest political party still active in Iran. Self-describes as "Muslims, Iranians, Constitutionalists and [Mossadegh](/people/mohammad-mossadegh)ists." Members of the Freedom Movement formed the core of the provisional government after the [1979 revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979), with [Bazargan](/people/mehdi-bazargan) as prime minister. Barred from elections since 1980 (with a brief exception in 2003 local elections). Despite being effectively outlawed, continues to exist as a "barely tolerated" opposition force. Splinter groups include the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) (1965) and the [Council of Nationalist-Religious Activists](/orgs/council-of-nationalist-religious-activists) (2000).
editorial_notes: Oldest active Iranian political party. Effectively banned but tolerated.
